title: Thinking in React
tagline: The official mental model for breaking UI into a component tree.

content.description: Before you write a single line of code, learn to see the interface as a tree of components. This is the mental shift that separates React beginners from React thinkers.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique walks you through the five-step process React engineers use to decompose any UI — from a simple mockup to a fully interactive component tree. By the end, you'll stop guessing where to put state and start reasoning about it.
content.quote: Kohai: a beginner technique. Walk this scroll at your own pace — the dojo is patient.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: useState: Managing Component State
tagline: Learn how to add and update local state inside your React components.

content.description: State is what makes a React component alive. Without it, your UI is just a static painting. With it, your components can remember, react, and respond.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique covers the useState hook from first principles — how to declare state, update it correctly, and avoid the most common mistakes beginners make. You'll also learn why mutating state directly is a silent killer.
content.quote: Kohai: a beginner technique. Every master was once a student who refused to skip the fundamentals.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: useEffect In Depth
tagline: Master side effects, dependencies, and cleanup in functional components.

content.description: useEffect is the most misunderstood hook in React. Most developers use it — few truly understand it. This scroll will change that.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique walks you through the mental model of synchronization — not lifecycle. You'll learn how the dependency array really works, when to use cleanup functions, and how to stop writing effects that fire twice, loop infinitely, or fetch stale data.
content.quote: Senpai: an intermediate technique. The dojo rewards those who slow down to understand, not just copy.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: React Router v6 Official Docs
tagline: The complete guide to client-side routing in modern React applications.

content.description: Single-page applications don't have pages — they have routes. React Router is the bridge between a URL and the component that should live there.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique covers the full React Router v6 API — from BrowserRouter and Routes, to dynamic params, nested routes, and the useNavigate and useSearchParams hooks. By the end, you'll build navigation that feels native.
content.quote: Senpai: an intermediate technique. Routes are the skeleton of your application — learn to build them strong.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: A Complete Guide to Flexbox
tagline: Everything you need to know about Flexbox, with visual examples.

content.description: Stop guessing which flex property does what. This is the definitive visual reference that every frontend developer has bookmarked — and for good reason.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique covers every Flexbox property from both the parent and child perspective. You'll finally understand the difference between justify-content and align-items, and why flex-grow behaves the way it does.
content.quote: Kohai: a beginner technique. Layout mastery begins with a single axis.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: A Complete Guide to CSS Grid
tagline: Master two-dimensional layouts with this comprehensive CSS Grid reference.

content.description: Flexbox handles one axis. CSS Grid handles two. Together, they give you complete control over any layout — no more float hacks, no more positioning nightmares.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique walks you through the full Grid API — grid-template-columns, grid-area, auto-placement, and the fr unit. You'll learn to build complex layouts that would have taken days with older CSS techniques.
content.quote: Kohai: a beginner technique. The grid is your canvas — learn to draw on it with intention.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: Responsive Design in CSS
tagline: Build layouts that adapt to any screen size using media queries.

content.description: Your users are on phones, tablets, laptops, and ultrawide monitors. Responsive design is not a feature — it is the baseline expectation of every modern web interface.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique covers the core principles of responsive design — fluid layouts, flexible images, and media queries. You'll learn the mobile-first approach and understand why min-width is preferred over max-width in modern CSS.
content.quote: Kohai: a beginner technique. A scroll that cannot be read on any device is a scroll left unread.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: CSS Custom Properties (Variables)
tagline: Use CSS variables to build consistent, themeable design systems.

content.description: Hardcoded color values scattered across thousands of CSS lines is a maintenance nightmare. CSS custom properties give you a single source of truth — change one value, update everything.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique covers how to declare, scope, and consume CSS variables — including dynamic theming, dark mode switching, and component-level overrides. You'll also learn how JavaScript can read and update CSS variables at runtime.
content.quote: Senpai: an intermediate technique. A consistent design system is the mark of a disciplined developer.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: JavaScript.info — The Modern JS Tutorial
tagline: From beginner to advanced — the most complete JS tutorial on the internet.

content.description: Not a crash course. Not a cheat sheet. A complete, thoughtfully written journey through the entire JavaScript language — from variables to closures to async programming.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique is a long-form training arc. You'll move through core syntax, functions, objects, prototypes, the DOM, events, and modern ES6+ features. Each chapter builds on the last — skip nothing.
content.quote: Kohai: a beginner technique. The longest scroll in the dojo. Those who finish it emerge changed.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: Async/Await & Promises Explained
tagline: Finally understand asynchronous JavaScript without losing your mind.

content.description: Asynchronous code is where most beginners hit a wall. Callbacks lead to promises, promises lead to async/await — and suddenly the wall disappears.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique walks you through the full async evolution in JavaScript. You'll understand the event loop, why promises were invented, and how async/await is syntactic sugar that makes asynchronous code read like synchronous code.
content.quote: Senpai: an intermediate technique. Time is an illusion — but in JavaScript, so is synchronous execution.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: Array Methods You Must Know
tagline: map, filter, reduce — the trio that transforms how you manipulate data.

content.description: forEach, map, filter, reduce, find, some, every — these are not optional knowledge. They are the vocabulary of modern JavaScript. Fluency here separates junior from mid-level developers.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique covers each essential array method with real examples. You'll learn not just what each method does, but when to reach for it — and why chaining them together is one of the most powerful patterns in JavaScript.
content.quote: Kohai: a beginner technique. Master the array, and you master half of JavaScript.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: Event Loop: How JS Really Works
tagline: Understand the call stack, web APIs, and microtask queue under the hood.

content.description: JavaScript is single-threaded — yet it handles timers, fetch calls, and user events simultaneously. The event loop is the mechanism that makes this possible. Understanding it changes how you write code.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique is a 30-minute video that visualizes the call stack, heap, web APIs, callback queue, and microtask queue in real time. You'll finally understand why setTimeout(fn, 0) doesn't always run immediately.
content.quote: Sensei: an advanced technique. The loop runs whether you understand it or not. The wise developer understands it.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: Node.js Official Getting Started
tagline: Run JavaScript on the server — set up your first Node.js project today.

content.description: JavaScript was born in the browser. Node.js set it free. With Node, the same language you use to build interfaces can now power servers, CLIs, and APIs.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique introduces the Node.js runtime — how it differs from browser JavaScript, how to run scripts, work with modules, and understand the Node ecosystem. It is the first step on the backend path.
content.quote: Kohai: a beginner technique. The server is just another dojo. Enter with an open mind.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: Build a REST API with Express.js
tagline: Create your first backend API with routes, middleware, and JSON responses.

content.description: Express is the minimalist framework that powers millions of Node.js APIs. It gets out of your way and lets you build — which means you need to understand what you are building.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique walks you through building a REST API from scratch — defining routes, handling HTTP methods, writing middleware, and returning JSON responses. By the end, you'll have a working API and the vocabulary to discuss backend architecture.
content.quote: Senpai: an intermediate technique. The frontend asks. The backend answers. Learn to give good answers.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.

title: JWT Authentication Explained
tagline: Secure your API with JSON Web Tokens — from theory to implementation.

content.description: Authentication is the gatekeeper of your application. JWT is the most widely used stateless authentication mechanism in modern APIs — and understanding it is non-negotiable for full-stack developers.
content.whatYouLearn: This technique covers the anatomy of a JSON Web Token — header, payload, and signature. You'll learn how tokens are issued, verified, and invalidated, and why stateless authentication scales better than session-based approaches.
content.quote: Sensei: an advanced technique. Security is not a feature you add later. It is a discipline you practice from the beginning.
content.pathForward: Open the source link to begin training. When you finish, return here and save the scroll — the way of the Ronin is to keep your collection tight, intentional, and revisitable.
